ClusterDescription: Homeodomain-containing transcription factors (homeobox family) that bind short TAAT/ATTA-like motifs; key regulators of embryonic patterning, regional identity and organogenesis.
HMG-box DNA-binding proteins (SOX family) that bend DNA and regulate chromatin accessibility; involved in development and cell fate decisions (neural, gonadal, stem cell maintenance).
TALE (three–amino-acid-loop-extension) homeodomain TFs (MEIS, PKNOX, TGIF) that often act as cofactors with HOX proteins to control developmental patterning and transcriptional specificity.
OTX/CRX/PITX family homeodomain TFs involved in cranial, retinal and sensory organ development and differentiation; bind homeodomain motifs and control tissue-specific gene programs.
POU-domain transcription factors (POU family: class I–V) that combine POU-specific and POU-homeodomain DNA binding to control cell identity (pluripotency, neural, pituitary).
Forkhead (FOX) winged-helix DNA-binding TFs that recognize core TGTTT/G motifs; regulate development, metabolism, and cell fate, including pioneer factors (FOXA) and lineage regulators (FOXP, FOXO).
AP-2 family transcription factors with a helix-span-helix DNA-binding domain; regulate neural crest, epidermal and developmental gene expression programs.
EBF family (helix-loop-helix / atypical zinc-finger architecture) transcription factors that bind palindromic motifs and direct B-cell, neuronal and olfactory differentiation.
RFX family winged-helix TFs that bind X-box motifs; important for ciliogenesis, immune regulation and tissue-specific gene expression.
Expanded AP-2 family cluster (including TFAP2E); similar DNA-binding and developmental roles as TFAP2A/B/C with tissue-specific activities.
ETS-domain TFs that recognize a core GGA(A/T) motif; regulate proliferation, differentiation and vascular / immune programs and frequently act with other TFs (e.g., AP-1, RUNX).
bZIP family AP-1 components (Fos, Jun, ATF, CREB-related) that form homo/heterodimers binding TRE/CRE motifs to regulate stress responses, proliferation and differentiation.
Class II bHLH neurogenic TFs (e.g., ATOH, NEUROG, OLIG) that bind E-box motifs and drive neuronal and lineage-specific differentiation programs.
Nuclear receptors (RARs and NR2F/COUP-TF) that bind retinoic acid response elements and heterodimerize with RXR to regulate development and differentiation.
Zinc-finger and SP family TFs (SP/MAZ) that bind GC-rich/GC-box elements; regulate housekeeping, growth and promoter-proximal transcription.
Large nuclear receptor group (ROR, RAR, RXR, PPAR, ERR, REV-ERB etc.) that bind hormone/response elements to control metabolism, circadian rhythm, development and differentiation.
Posterior/late-expressed HOX homeodomain TFs involved in distal/late developmental patterning and tissue specification.
bHLH and bHLH-ZIP TFs (CLOCK, ARNT/HIF, MYC/MAX, bHLH-PER/HEY family) that bind E-box motifs and regulate circadian rhythm, hypoxia, proliferation and metabolism.
Extended AP-1 / bZIP family including BATF/MAF/NRF modules that heterodimerize with Jun/Fos and modulate immune, oxidative stress and differentiation responses.
E2F family transcription factors that bind E2F motifs and control cell-cycle entry, DNA replication and S-phase gene expression.
Myogenic and neurogenic bHLH TFs (MYF family, ASCL, NEUROD, TCFs) that bind E-box sites to regulate muscle and neuronal lineage commitment and differentiation.
ZBTB7 (POK family) zinc-finger TFs that combine BTB/POZ and C2H2 zinc-finger domains; function as transcriptional repressors in development and oncogenesis.
KLF zinc-finger and SP family TFs binding GC/GT boxes; regulate proliferation, differentiation, and housekeeping gene expression.
T-box DNA-binding domain TFs (TBX family) that control mesodermal, cardiac and developmental programs through T-element binding.
REL/NF-κB family Rel-homology domain TFs that bind κB sites to regulate immune, inflammatory and stress response genes.
E2F6/E2F8 and DP family members — atypical E2Fs and DP partners that regulate cell-cycle transcription and transcriptional repression complexes.
GATA zinc-finger TF family binding WGATAR motifs; master regulators of hematopoiesis, endoderm and cardiac development.
Heat shock factor family (HSF) with helix-turn-helix and oligomerization domains; activate heat-shock/chaperone gene expression in stress responses.
Mixed cluster with EMT zinc-finger TFs (SNAI, ZEB) and bHLH factors (TCFs, ASCL) — regulators of epithelial-to-mesenchymal transition and lineage plasticity.
MADS-box/MEF2 family TFs that bind MEF2 sites and regulate muscle, neuronal and developmental gene programs.
ATF (bZIP) and C/EBP (bZIP) family mix — transcription factors binding CRE/CAAT motifs to regulate stress responses, metabolism and differentiation.
ETS/ETV factors fused or paired with FIGLA/other partners — likely reflect composite motifs where ETS and oocyte/FIGLA-type factors co-occur (developmental/germline contexts).
CREB/ATF family bZIP TFs (CREB3, ATF6, XBP1) that bind CRE/XBP motifs and regulate unfolded protein response, stress and metabolic programs.
IRF family (Interferon Regulatory Factors) with helix-turn-helix/DBD that bind ISRE/IRF motifs to control antiviral, immune differentiation and interferon signaling.
TEAD (TEA/ATTS) family transcription factors that bind MCAT/TEAD motifs and function with YAP/TAZ to regulate growth, organ size and mechanotransduction.
Krüppel-like/GLIS zinc-finger family and VEZF1 — zinc-finger TFs implicated in vascular development, kidney function and transcriptional regulation.
EGR (early growth response) zinc-finger TFs that bind GC-rich EGR sites and mediate immediate early responses in growth, differentiation and neuronal activity.
bHLH TFs (NEUROD, ATOH, TAL1) and TWIST family basic helix-loop-helix regulators that control neuronal differentiation and mesenchymal/EMT programs.
BARHL family homeodomain TFs important in neuronal differentiation and survival (homeobox DNA-binding, cerebellar/retinal development).
Grainyhead-like (GRHL) and TFCP2 family TFs that regulate epithelial differentiation, barrier function and morphogenesis.
SREBP (SREBF) bHLH-ZIP transcription factors that bind sterol regulatory elements to control lipid biosynthesis and cholesterol homeostasis.
HNF4 nuclear receptor family with zinc-finger DNA-binding domain; master regulators of liver, intestinal gene expression and metabolism.
CTCF zinc-finger insulator protein that binds diverse motifs to organize chromatin looping, insulation and higher-order genome architecture.
PBX and PKNOX TALE homeodomain cofactors that heterodimerize with MEIS/HOX and modulate developmental transcriptional specificity.
OVOL C2H2 zinc-finger TFs that repress mesenchymal programs and promote epithelial differentiation / MET during development and tumorigenesis.
NFAT family (Rel-like/Calcineurin-regulated) transcription factors that translocate to nucleus following calcium signaling to control immune activation and development.
p53/p63/p73 tumor suppressor family with sequence-specific DNA-binding domain; regulate DNA damage response, apoptosis, development and differentiation.
CDX homeobox TFs with posterior axial patterning roles together with specific HOX members; control intestinal fate and anterior–posterior patterning.
PAR-bZIP family (DBP, TEF) bZIP TFs involved in circadian regulation and rhythmic transcriptional programs.
STAT family (SH2-mediated cytokine signaling) transcription factors that bind GAS elements to mediate cytokine responses, immune differentiation and growth signaling.
SIX homeobox TFs (SIX1/2/4) that bind homeodomain-related motifs and regulate craniofacial, muscle and sensory organ development.
SCRT (Scratch) zinc-finger TFs expressed in neural contexts; involved in neuronal differentiation and migration.
HNF1 homeodomain-related TFs with a unique POU-like dimerization domain that regulate liver, kidney and pancreatic gene programs.
Zinc-finger (ZNF) and SP-family like cluster including SP5 — GC-box binding regulators involved in developmental and promoter-proximal gene control.
C/EBP family bZIP TFs that bind CCAAT/enhancer elements to regulate adipogenesis, myelopoiesis and inflammatory responses.
TALE homeodomain cofactors (MEIS, PBX) that form complexes with HOX proteins to specify developmental transcriptional outputs.
NKX2 family homeodomain TFs that regulate tissue-specific programs in heart, neural and endodermal development.
NFKB family members (NFKB1/NFKB2) central to canonical and noncanonical NF-κB signaling controlling immunity, inflammation and cell survival.
NR4A orphan nuclear receptors (immediate-early response NRs) that bind NBRE elements to regulate metabolism, apoptosis and stress responses.
PAX family paired-box/homeodomain TFs that combine paired and homeobox modules to control organogenesis, kidney and sensory development.
Nuclear Factor I family (NFIA/B/C/X) with unique DBD that bind the CCAAT-like NF-I motif and regulate brain, lung and developmental gene expression.
GLI zinc-finger TFs that mediate Hedgehog signaling by binding GACCACCCA-like motifs to control patterning and proliferation.
Large MAF family and NRL bZIP TFs that bind MAF-recognition elements to regulate differentiation in liver, lens and photoreceptors.
NKX3 family homeodomain TFs with roles in prostate and developmental patterning; act as transcriptional regulators of differentiation.
RUNX family (Runt domain) and BCL11 TFs — DNA-binding factors involved in hematopoiesis, neurodevelopment and chromatin regulation.
TCF/LEF HMG-box TFs that bind Wnt/β-catenin responsive elements to mediate Wnt signaling and control stem cell and developmental programs.
ZIC family zinc-finger/homeodomain-related TFs that regulate neural development, patterning and cerebellar differentiation.
C/EBPα and C/EBPδ bZIP TFs that regulate differentiation, metabolism and inflammatory gene programs; C/EBPα is a key myeloid/adipogenic regulator.
AP-2 family transcription factors (TFAP2A/B/C) controlling neural crest, epidermal and developmental transcription programs.
bZIP family regulators HLF and NFIL3 that contribute to circadian and immune-related transcriptional rhythms.
NF-Y trimeric CCAAT-box binding complex (NFYA/NFYC etc.) that recognizes CCAAT motifs and acts as a general transcriptional regulator.
RFX family members (RFX4/6) — winged-helix TFs implicated in brain and pancreatic development and ciliogenesis-associated transcription.
HOX13 paralogs (HOXB13, HOXA13, HOXD13) that specify distal/posterior identities during development and control limb/genital patterning.
SMAD family mediators of TGF-β/BMP signaling that bind GC-rich Smad-binding elements to regulate development, differentiation and cell-cycle responses.
The Early B-cell Factor (EBF) family of transcription factors.
CUT/ONECUT homeodomain TFs (CUX, ONECUT) that control epithelial differentiation, liver and pancreas development and chromatin architecture.
ZIC family members (ZIC2/3) involved in neural tube and forebrain development; zinc-finger/homeodomain-related DNA binding.
PITX homeobox TFs (and DMBX1) involved in left-right asymmetry, ocular/forebrain development and organogenesis.
GMEB family transcriptional modulators that interact with other TFs and may influence glucocorticoid receptor / chromatin responses.
Steroid hormone nuclear receptors (AR, GR/Nr3c1, MR/NR3C2, PR) with zinc-finger DBDs controlling endocrine, metabolic and reproductive gene programs.
NKX2 family homeodomain TFs (NKX2-2, NKX2-5) that regulate neural and cardiac development and tissue-specific differentiation.
